DP.046.0000:
[0103,0000]: Whoops!
Hey, [0103,0001]!\f
You’re going to challenge the Gym
Leader up past here, eh?\f
Someone should check to see if you’re
ready for this challenge!\f
That someone is me!
With my brand-new Gym Badge!\f
DP.046.0001:
[0103,0000]: Yeah, yeah, you’re just
a bit better than me, as usual.\f
But listen up!\f
Let me tell you who’s going to take
on the Pokémon League and become\r
the Champion.\f
You guessed right!
It’s going to be me!\f
Anyways, you should be able to
take the Gym Leader here.\f
Hurry up and go make the challenge!\f
DP.046.0002:
[0103,0000]: Hey! That’s the Mine
Badge! Good going!\f
You’re tough enough to consider
becoming the Pokémon Champ.\f
Of course, I’m tougher, so it’s
not going to actually happen.\f
Anyways, [0103,0001], come with
me to the library.\f
DP.046.0003:
Rowan: !\f
...That tremor...
That wasn’t a natural occurrence.\f
DP.046.0004:
Hey, hey! Old dude and kids!
Something big just happened!\f
They’re saying there was a huge
explosion at Lake Valor!\f
[FF01,00C8]Ka-boom!![FF01,0064]\f
[FF01,00C8][FF01,0064]Like that!\f
DP.046.0005:
[0103,0000]: Gramps!\f
I’m going to the lake!
I have a bad feeling about this!\f
DP.046.0006:
Rowan: !\f
...That [0103,0000]...
Can’t sit still...\f
[0103,0001], I’m worried about
Lake Valor.\f
But I have no idea what is taking
place. You must find out!\f
But before you go, let me stress:
don’t get in over your head.\f
We will try to join you as soon as
we inspect Lake Verity.\f
DP.046.0007:
Dawn: ...[0103,0000], you’ll be OK,
won’t you?
DP.046.0008:
Lucas: Lake Valor...
I wonder what happened?
DP.046.0009:
Dawn: We’re going off to Lake Verity
first...\f
Don’t do anything rash if something
does happen.
DP.046.0010:
Lucas: We’re going to investigate
Lake Verity before we join you.\f
Don’t try to be a hero if anything
crazy happens.
DP.046.0011:
Rowan: Hmm...\f
What is taking place at Lake
Valor... You must find out!\f
But before you go, let me stress:
don’t get in over your head.\f
We will try to join you as soon as
we inspect Lake Verity.
DP.046.0012:
What’s up?
Your clothes look damp.\f
Were you swimming at the beach
right by Sandgem Town?
DP.046.0013:
The tremor earlier...
It didn’t feel like an earthquake.\f
It felt more like an explosion.
DP.046.0014:
Welcome to Canalave City, the Canal
City!\f
In the old days, this port used to
be a hub of cargo transportation.
DP.046.0015:
What was that tremor?
I thought Mt. Coronet erupted!
DP.046.0016:
I’m going to study at the library.
I love reading.\f
I love how reading takes me to places
far away in time and space.
DP.046.0017:
An explosion at Lake Valor?
But there isn’t anything there?
DP.046.0018:
My PSYDUCK is very close to me.
It can use the move Return!
DP.046.0019:
E-even PS-PSYDUCK’s Future Sight
couldn’t foretell that tremor!
DP.046.0021:
PSYDUCK: Gua! Gua! Gwa?
Guaa! Guuagwa?!
DP.046.0022:
The Gym Leader and Trainers of this Gym
often go out to Iron Island.\f
They not only train their Pokémon,
but they work out as well.
DP.046.0026:
You just tell me whenever you need
to set sail!
DP.046.0027:
Ahoy! You just go on and tell me
whenever you want to set sail again!
DP.046.0028:
I’ve lost my love of sailing since my
son fell into that horrid nightmare...\f
I need the Lunar Wing from Fullmoon
Island to awaken him!\f
Would you go to Fullmoon Island for me?
If not for me, then for my son?
DP.046.0029:
We’re both counting on you!\f
Only you can awaken my son from his
awful nightmare...\f
Please, I need the Lunar Wing from
Fullmoon Island to awaken him!\f
DP.046.0030:
Do you need to get ready?\f
I’ll be waiting here.
We’re desperate for your help.
DP.046.0031:
That Lunar Wing...
Please! Take it to my son...
DP.046.0032:
You’ve been asleep for a long,
long time... What happened to you?\f
And what were you doing here?
No one’s lived here for fifty years.\f
DP.046.0034:
Canalave City Pokémon Gym
Leader: Byron\r
The Man with the Steel Body!
DP.046.0038:
Harbor Inn...
The text is too faded to read.
DP.046.0039:
Harbor Inn
A Roost for Restless Sailors\f
“Enjoy sweet dreams in our
sumptuous beds!”
DP.046.0040:
The door is tightly shut.
It appears impossible to open.
